183→ def branch_manager(self) -> BranchManager: 184→ """Get the appropriate BranchManager for branch operations. 185→ 186→ Returns GitBranchManager when Graphite is disabled, 187→ GraphiteBranchManager when Graphite is enabled. 188→ 189→ This provides a unified interface for branch operations that 190→ handles Graphite vs plain Git differences transparently. 191→ """ 192→ # Check if Graphite is disabled (also handles DryRunGraphite wrapping GraphiteDisabled) 193→ graphite = self.graphite 194→ if isinstance(graphite, DryRunGraphite): 195→ graphite = graphite._wrapped 196→ if isinstance(graphite, GraphiteDisabled): 197→ return GitBranchManager( 198→ git=self.git, 199→ github=self.github, 200→ ) 201→ if self.graphite_branch_ops is None: 202→ raise RuntimeError("graphite_branch_ops must be set when Graphite is enabled") 203→ return GraphiteBranchManager( 204→ git=self.git, 205→ graphite=self.graphite, 206→ graphite_branch_ops=self.graphite_branch_ops, 207→ github=self.github, 208→ ) 209→ 210→ @staticmethod 211→ def for_test( 212→ *,

session.assistant:
The `branch_manager` property is computed, deriving from `graphite`/`graphite_branch_ops`. To get FakeBranchManager behavior, I need to either: 1. Use FakeGraphite that delegates to FakeBranchManager 2. Or use GraphiteBranchManager with FakeGraphite configured correctly
Since the test pattern wants to control stacks directly, the cleanest approach is to make my tests use FakeGraphite with the right configuration, or to write a helper that replaces the property. But actually — the simplest approach is to pass FakeGraphite with the right branches configured. Let me look at how FakeGraphite works:
session.tool_result:
1→"""Fake Graphite operations for testing. 2→ 3→FakeGraphite is an in-memory implementation that accepts pre-configured state 4→in its constructor. Construct instances directly with keyword arguments. 5→""" 6→ 7→from __future__ import annotations 8→ 9→from pathlib import Path 10→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 11→ 12→if TYPE_CHECKING: 13→ from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.branch_ops.fake import FakeGraphiteBranchOps 14→ 15→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import Git 16→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId, PullRequestInfo 17→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.abc import Graphite 18→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.types import BranchMetadata 19→ 20→ 21→class FakeGraphite(Graphite): 22→ """In-memory fake implementation of Graphite operations. 23→ 24→ This class has NO public setup methods. All state is provided via constructor 25→ using keyword arguments with sensible defaults (empty dicts). 26→ """ 27→ 28→ def __init__( 29→ self, 30→ *,

session.assistant:
The `branch_manager` property derives from `graphite` — when FakeGraphite is used, it creates `GraphiteBranchManager`. The issue is that `GraphiteBranchManager` will delegate stack/track operations to graphite itself, not to FakeBranchManager.
So in my tests I need to use FakeGraphite with the right stacks configured and use context_for_test with `graphite=FakeGraphite(stacks={...})`. Let me also check what `GraphiteBranchManager.track_branch` and `is_graphite_managed` do:
session.tool_result:
217→ def track_branch(self, repo_root: Path, branch_name: str, parent_branch: str) -> None: 218→ """Track an existing branch with Graphite. 219→ 220→ Registers the branch with Graphite for stack tracking. 221→ 222→ Args: 223→ repo_root: Repository root directory 224→ branch_name: Name of the branch to track 225→ parent_branch: Name of the parent branch 226→ """ 227→ self.graphite_branch_ops.track_branch(repo_root, branch_name, parent_branch) 228→ 229→ def retrack_branch(self, repo_root: Path, branch_name: str) -> None: 230→ """Re-track a branch to fix Graphite tracking divergence. 231→ 232→ Delegates to GraphiteBranchOps.retrack_branch which runs 233→ `gt track <branch> --no-interactive`. 234→ 235→ Args: 236→ repo_root: Repository root directory 237→ branch_name: Name of the branch to re-track 238→ """ 239→ self.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(repo_root, branch_name) 240→ 241→ def get_parent_branch(self, repo_root: Path, branch: str) -> str | None: 242→ """Get parent branch from Graphite's cache. 243→ 244→ Args: 245→ repo_root: Repository root directory 246→ branch: Name of the branch to get the parent for
... [55 lines omitted] ...
